resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1) Modify the existing activity "provide customer brochurs" by adding output/write data object "customer_brochures". 2) Keep the existing resource assignment "customer_advisor" unchanged.

compliance_degree.reason: The existing activity already executed by "customer_advisor" becomes an activity that sends or produces "customer_brochures", satisfying the violated executed_by requirement for the send activity.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: No control-flow, ordering, condition, or resource assignment relevant to R1, R4, R6, or R7 is changed. Existing compliant behavior is preserved.

change_cost.reason: Only a data-output annotation is added to an existing activity. No new activity or restructuring is required.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The structured tree remains unchanged. The modification only enriches the data semantics of an existing activity.

change_location.reason: The changed activity occurs near the beginning of the process, immediately after "new customer arrives".

resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1) Rename the existing activity "provide customer brochurs" to "provide customer brochures" or "send customer brochures" to clarify its purpose. 2) Add output/write data object "customer_brochures" to that activity. 3) Preserve the existing resource assignment "customer_advisor".

compliance_degree.reason: The renamed activity explicitly represents sending or providing brochures, produces "customer_brochures", and remains executed by "customer_advisor".

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The change does not alter the process order, branches, conditions, or the activities involved in the context requirements. R1, R4, R6, and R7 remain unaffected.

change_cost.reason: The strategy requires a label correction and a data-output annotation, but no structural change.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The activity name becomes clearer and the data semantics become explicit. The process structure is not modified.

change_location.reason: The affected activity is located at the start of the process, directly after customer arrival.

resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1) Insert a new activity "send customer brochures" directly after "provide customer brochurs" and before the following exclusive branch. 2) Assign resource "customer_advisor" to the new activity. 3) Add output/write data object "customer_brochures" to the new activity.

compliance_degree.reason: The inserted activity explicitly sends or produces "customer_brochures" and is executed by "customer_advisor", satisfying the violated requirement.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The insertion is before the existing exclusive branch and does not affect the ordering or execution conditions required by R1, R4, R6, or R7.

change_cost.reason: A new activity is added, requiring changes to the process model and possibly operational execution, even though no complex restructuring is needed.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The sequence remains well-structured, but the process gains an additional task that may be redundant with the existing brochure-provision activity.

change_location.reason: The new activity is inserted near the beginning of the process, before the first exclusive branch.

best_resolution_reason: RS1 is the preferred strategy because it fully restores compliance with the smallest possible localized change. The existing activity is already performed by "customer_advisor" and semantically appears to provide brochures, so adding the missing "customer_brochures" output is sufficient. It preserves all resolution context requirements and has no structural impact.
